serviceDescription: AWS Migration Hub Refactor Spaces is the starting point for incremental application refactoring to microservices. Refactor Spaces eliminates the undifferentiated work of building and operating AWS infrastructure for incremental refactoring. You can use Refactor Spaces to reduce the risk of evolving applications into microservices or extending existing applications with new features written in microservices.

templates.title: Migration Hub Services
templates.helpText: AWS Migration Hub Refactor Spaces is the starting point for incremental application refactoring to microservices. Refactor Spaces eliminates the undifferentiated work of building and operating AWS infrastructure for incremental refactoring. You can use Refactor Spaces to reduce the risk of evolving applications into microservices or extending existing applications with new features written in microservices.

cards.title: Migration Hub Refactor Spaces
cards.description: With Refactor Spaces, you pay only for what you use. There are no upfront costs or minimum fees. You are charged based on the number of hours that you run your refactor environments and API requests to Refactor Spaces.
cards.helpText: AWS Migration Hub Refactor Spaces is the starting point for incremental application refactoring to microservices. Refactor Spaces eliminates the undifferentiated work of building and operating AWS infrastructure for incremental refactoring. You can use Refactor Spaces to reduce the risk of evolving applications into microservices or extending existing applications with new features written in microservices.

components.text: The Refactor Spaces environment provides a unified view of networking, applications, and services across multiple AWS accounts. You are charged based on the number of environment hours that are used.

The API reference provides descriptions, syntax, and other details about each of the actions and data types for AWS Migration Hub Refactor Spaces (Refactor Spaces). You are charged based on the volume of API requests you make per month.

Select the units and number for API requests based on expected volume (first 500,000 API requests are free per month).

components.label: API Requests
components.placeholder: Enter number of API requests in units selected

cards.title: Transit Gateway
cards.helpText: AWS Transit Gateway is a service that enables customers to connect their Amazon Virtual Private Clouds (VPCs) and their on-premises networks to a single gateway. Transit Gateway per hour and the amount of traffic that flows through AWS Transit Gateway are charged. You can create a Transit Gateway to connect to multiple Amazon Virtual Private Clouds (Amazon VPCs), Direct Connect and VPNs, and that Transit Gateway can work across accounts. The VPC account owner is billed hourly for each hour that their Amazon VPCs attached to an AWS Transit Gateway.

components.text: Number of Transit Gateway attachments is equal to the number of Refactor Spaces Environments

components.label: Data processed per Transit Gateway attachment

meteredUnit.allRegions: Hourly charge for Transit Gateway VPC Attachments per hour

components.label: price for Transit Gateway data processing

meteredUnit.allRegions: Charge for per GB data processed by AWS Transit Gateways for VPC attachments per GigaBytes

components.content: You will incur charges for any AWS service provisioned by Refactor Spaces.  AWS Refactor Spaces bridges networking across your AWS accounts by orchestrating AWS Resource Access Manager, AWS Transit Gateway, API Gateway, Network Load Balancer and VPCs to let your existing application and the new services communicate directly.  When you create an environment, Refactor Spaces creates a Transit Gateway (TGW) for you to manage the communication between your monolith and microservices.  These resources are orchestrated inside your AWS account, so you also pay for your usage.

operands.variableLabel: Data processed per Transit Gateway attachment
price for Transit Gateway data processing

components.outputUnitLabel: [currency] (Transit Gateway processing and monthly cost per attachment)

operands.variableLabel: [currency] (Transit Gateway processing and monthly cost per attachment)

cards.title: API Gateway
cards.helpText: Amazon API Gateway is a fully managed service that makes it easy for developers to create, publish, maintain, monitor, and secure APIs at any scale. APIs act as the front door for applications to access data, business logic, or functionality from your backend services.

components.text: Select the units, number, and frequency for REST API requests based on expected volume. The calculations below exclude free tier discounts.

components.label: Rest Requests
components.placeholder: Enter number of REST API requests in units selected

components.label: Cache memory size (GB)
components.description: Optional. Choose cache memory size in GB. Dedicated cache memory is billed at an hourly rate.

allRegions.meteredUnit: API Calls Number of up to 333 million
API Calls Number of Request Next 667 million
API Calls Number of Request Next 19 billion
API Calls Number of Request Over 20 billion

components.content: You will incur charges for any AWS service provisioned by Refactor Spaces.  Refactor Spaces simplifies operating an application across multiple accounts by orchestrating AWS Transit Gateway, VPCs, Amazon API Gateway, Network Load Balancer, AWS Resource Access Manager, and resource policies so that you can transparently add new services to an external HTTP endpoint.  These resources are orchestrated inside your AWS account, so you also pay for your usage.

components.label: Tiered price total for REST API requests

cards.title: Network Load Balancer
cards.description: You are charged for each hour or partial hour that a Network Load Balancer is running and the number of Load Balancer Capacity Units (LCU) used by Network Load Balancer per hour.
cards.helpText: An LCU measures the dimensions on which the Network Load Balancer processes your traffic (averaged over an hour). The three dimensions measured are: new connections or flows, active connections or flows, and bandwidth. You are charged only on one of the three dimensions that has the highest usage for the hour.

components.text:
An LCU measures the dimensions on which the Network Load Balancer processes your traffic (averaged over an hour).
Please specify at least one of the following dimensions to determine LCU pricing

components.label: Processed bytes per NLB for TCP
Average number of new TCP connections
Average TCP connection duration
components.description: Enter the average duration for each new connection (if the duration is less than 1 second then enter 1 second).

components.label: pricing for nlb lcu per hour
Processed bytes per NLB for UDP
Average number of new UDP Flows
Average UDP Flow duration
components.description: Enter the average duration for each new Flow (if the duration is less than 1 second then enter 1 second).

components.label: pricing for nlb lcu per hour
Processed bytes per NLB for TLS
Average number of new TLS connections
Average TLS connection duration
components.description: Enter the average duration for each new connection (if the duration is less than 1 second then enter 1 second).

components.label: pricing for nlb lcu per hour

components.content: You will incur charges for any AWS service provisioned by Refactor Spaces. AWS Refactor Spaces bridges networking across your AWS accounts by orchestrating AWS Resource Access Manager, AWS Transit Gateway, API Gateway, Network Load Balancer and VPCs to let your existing application and the new services communicate directly. When you create an environment, Refactor Spaces creates a Network Load Balancer for you to manage the communication between your monolith and microservices. Refactor Spaces will create one Network Load Balancer for each application you create.  This  means that within the environment if you have 4 applications, you will have 4 Network Load Balancers.  These resources are orchestrated inside your AWS account, so you also pay for your usage.

components.label: Total hourly charges for all Network Load Balancers
